steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is an Inferences question. The passage presents two related findings: (1) black-furred wolves are more common in areas with frequent distemper outbreaks, and (2) black-furred wolves are more likely to be immune to distemper than gray-furred wolves. The blank must complete the conclusion that most logically follows from both findings taken together.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — North American gray wolves experience more outbreaks of distemper virus than other wolf species do.: The passage compares black-furred and gray-furred wolves within the same species. No comparison to other wolf species is made or supported. ✗
  • Choice B — the gene variant that results in black fur may be linked to immunity to the distemper virus.: This ties both findings together: if the same gene variant that causes black fur is also associated with immunity, it explains why black wolves are more common where distemper is frequent — they survive outbreaks at higher rates. This is the most logical conclusion from the combined evidence. ✓
  • Choice C — the average life span of gray wolves is likely to increase over time because of a particular gene variant.: The passage says nothing about life spans increasing, and the gene variant described is associated with black fur and immunity — not with changes to gray wolf longevity. ✗
  • Choice D — gray-furred wolves will soon replace black-furred wolves across North America.: The evidence points in the opposite direction: black wolves with immunity have a survival advantage in distemper-prone areas. There is no support for gray wolves replacing black ones. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
